nodes.name: Countries of the World
nodes.description: Country sets by region and category

items.prompt: The world's most populous country since 2023 with ~1.44 billion people, having recently surpassed its northern neighbor. Name this South Asian nation.
items.explanation:
#1 most populous country — ~1.44 billion (surpassed China in 2023)
Capital New Delhi; 28 states and 8 union territories
World's largest democracy by population
Projected to reach ~1.7 billion by 2060 before declining
Home to 22 officially recognized languages and hundreds more

items.prompt: Held the title of world's most populous country for centuries until 2023, still home to ~1.43 billion people. Name this East Asian nation whose population is now declining.
items.explanation:
#2 most populous country — ~1.43 billion (lost #1 spot to India in 2023)
Population peaked around 2022 and is now shrinking; one-child policy (1980–2015) drastically reduced birth rates
Capital Beijing; world's second-largest economy by nominal GDP
Borders 14 countries — tied with Russia for the most land borders of any nation
Home to the Great Wall, a UNESCO site stretching ~21,000 km across northern regions

items.prompt: The third most populous country at ~340 million, this North American nation is the most populous in the Western Hemisphere. Name it.
items.explanation:
#3 most populous country — ~340 million
Capital Washington, D.C.; 50 states spanning 6 time zones in the contiguous area
World's largest economy by nominal GDP (~$28 trillion)
Population grew largely through immigration; about 14% of residents are foreign-born
Third-largest country by both area (~9.8 million km²) and population

items.prompt: The world's fourth most populous country at ~280 million and the largest archipelago nation, spanning over 17,000 islands in Southeast Asia. Name it.
items.explanation:
#4 most populous country — ~280 million
World's largest archipelago: over 17,000 islands, ~6,000 inhabited
Capital officially moved from Jakarta to Nusantara (new planned city in Borneo) starting 2024
World's most populous Muslim-majority country
Fourth-largest island in the world (Borneo) is shared among three countries including this one

items.prompt: The fifth most populous country at ~240 million, this South Asian nation was created by partition in 1947 and shares a contested border region with its eastern neighbor. Name it.
items.explanation:
#5 most populous country — ~240 million
Created by the Partition of British India in 1947; originally included East Pakistan (now Bangladesh)
Capital Islamabad (purpose-built, replacing Karachi in 1967)
K2, the world's second-highest mountain (8,611 m), sits on its border with China
One of nine countries with nuclear weapons; tested its first device in 1998

items.prompt: Africa's most populous country at ~230 million, this West African nation is named after the river that flows through it and is a major oil producer. Name it.
items.explanation:
#6 most populous country — ~230 million (most populous in Africa by a wide margin)
Named after the Niger River; capital Abuja (moved from Lagos in 1991)
Africa's largest economy; one of the top oil producers globally (OPEC member)
Over 250 ethnic groups and 500+ languages; English is the official language
Projected to become the world's third most populous country by 2050

items.prompt: The seventh most populous country at ~216 million and the most populous in South America, this Portuguese-speaking nation contains most of the Amazon rainforest. Name it.
items.explanation:
#7 most populous country — ~216 million (most populous in Latin America and the Southern Hemisphere)
Only Portuguese-speaking country in the Americas; capital Brasília (purpose-built, inaugurated 1960)
Contains about 60% of the Amazon rainforest, the world's largest tropical rainforest
Fifth-largest country by area (~8.5 million km²)
Record five FIFA World Cup titles (1958, 1962, 1970, 1994, 2002)

items.prompt: The eighth most populous country at ~173 million, this South Asian nation was formerly the eastern wing of a neighboring country until gaining independence in 1971. Name it.
items.explanation:
#8 most populous country — ~173 million in just ~148,000 km² (one of the most densely populated countries on Earth)
Formerly East Pakistan; gained independence in 1971 after a liberation war
Capital Dhaka; sits on the Ganges–Brahmaputra delta, making it extremely vulnerable to flooding
World's second-largest garment exporter after China
Bengali is the national language — the same language spoken in India's West Bengal state

items.prompt: The ninth most populous country at ~144 million, this transcontinental nation is by far the world's largest by area, spanning 11 time zones. Name it.
items.explanation:
#9 most populous country — ~144 million (modest population for its enormous size)
World's largest country by area at ~17.1 million km², spanning 11 time zones
Capital Moscow; population heavily concentrated west of the Ural Mountains
Permanent member of the UN Security Council; world's largest nuclear arsenal
Lake Baikal, in Siberia, holds about 20% of the world's unfrozen surface fresh water

items.prompt: The tenth most populous country at ~130 million, this landlocked East African nation is the only country on the continent never formally colonized by a European power. Name it.
items.explanation:
#10 most populous country — ~130 million (second most populous in Africa after Nigeria)
Never colonized — repelled Italian invasion at the Battle of Adwa (1896); occupied briefly 1936–1941
Capital Addis Ababa, which serves as headquarters of the African Union
Uses the Ethiopian calendar, which is ~7–8 years behind the Gregorian calendar
Birthplace of coffee; the Coffea arabica plant originated in its highland forests

items.prompt: The eleventh most populous country at ~129 million, this North American nation has the world's largest Spanish-speaking population. Name it.
items.explanation:
#11 most populous country — ~129 million
World's largest Spanish-speaking country by population
Capital Mexico City (~22 million metro), built on the ruins of the Aztec capital Tenochtitlán
Third-largest country in Latin America by area after Brazil and Argentina
Home to 35 UNESCO World Heritage Sites, among the highest totals worldwide
Chichen Itzá's El Castillo pyramid is one of the New Seven Wonders of the World

items.prompt: The twelfth most populous country at ~124 million, this East Asian island nation has the world's oldest population by median age. Name it.
items.explanation:
#12 most populous country — ~124 million (declining; peaked at ~128 million in 2010)
World's highest median age (~49 years) and one of the lowest birth rates globally
Capital Tokyo (~14 million city, ~37 million metro — world's largest metropolitan area)
World's fourth-largest economy by nominal GDP
Archipelago of 6,852 islands; about 73% of the land is mountainous and forested

items.prompt: The thirteenth most populous country at ~117 million, this Southeast Asian archipelago of over 7,000 islands was named after a 16th-century Spanish king. Name it.
items.explanation:
#13 most populous country — ~117 million
Named after King Philip II of Spain; Spanish colony for 333 years (1565–1898)
Capital Manila; Metro Manila has ~14 million people
Archipelago of 7,641 islands; the three main island groups are Luzon, Visayas, and Mindanao
Only predominantly Christian nation in Asia (~86% Catholic)
U.S. territory from 1898 to 1946; gained full independence on July 4, 1946

items.prompt: The fourteenth most populous country at ~110 million, this North African nation is home to the only surviving Ancient Wonder of the World. Name it.
items.explanation:
#14 most populous country — ~110 million (most populous in the Arab world)
The Great Pyramid of Giza is the only surviving Wonder of the Ancient World
Capital Cairo (~22 million metro); virtually all the population lives along the Nile River and its delta
The Suez Canal (opened 1869) connects the Mediterranean and Red Seas
Ancient Egyptian civilization lasted ~3,000 years (c. 3100 BCE–332 BCE)

items.prompt: The fifteenth most populous country at ~105 million, this Central African nation is the continent's second-largest by area and shares its name with a neighboring republic. Name it.
items.explanation:
#15 most populous country — ~105 million
Second-largest country in Africa by area (~2.3 million km²)
Capital Kinshasa; distinguished from the Republic of the Congo (capital Brazzaville) across the river
Formerly Zaire (1971–1997) under dictator Mobutu Sese Seko
Contains vast mineral wealth including cobalt (~70% of global supply), crucial for lithium-ion batteries
The Congo River is the world's deepest river and second-largest by discharge

items.prompt: The sixteenth most populous country at ~100 million, this Southeast Asian nation stretches along the eastern coast of the Indochinese Peninsula and was reunified in 1975. Name it.
items.explanation:
#16 most populous country — ~100 million
Reunified in 1975 after the fall of Saigon ended the Vietnam War (known locally as the American War)
Capital Hanoi; Ho Chi Minh City (formerly Saigon) is the largest city and economic hub
One of five remaining communist one-party states
Ha Long Bay, with ~1,600 limestone islands, is a UNESCO World Heritage Site
World's second-largest coffee exporter (after Brazil), specializing in robusta beans

items.prompt: The seventeenth most populous country at ~90 million, this West Asian nation was known internationally by a different name until 1935 and became an Islamic republic after a 1979 revolution. Name it.
items.explanation:
#17 most populous country — ~90 million
Formerly known as Persia internationally; asked to be called Iran in 1935
Became an Islamic republic after the 1979 revolution that overthrew Shah Mohammad Reza Pahlavi
Capital Tehran (~9 million city, ~16 million metro)
Sits on the world's second-largest natural gas reserves and fourth-largest oil reserves
Persepolis, ceremonial capital of the Achaemenid Empire (c. 515 BCE), is a UNESCO World Heritage Site

items.prompt: The eighteenth most populous country at ~86 million, this transcontinental nation straddles Europe and Asia with its largest city split between two continents by a famous strait. Name it.
items.explanation:
#18 most populous country — ~86 million
Transcontinental: ~97% in Asia (Anatolia), ~3% in Europe (Thrace), divided by the Bosphorus
Capital Ankara (not Istanbul); Istanbul (~16 million) is the largest city and straddles two continents
Successor to the Ottoman Empire (1299–1922); republic founded by Mustafa Kemal Atatürk in 1923
Hagia Sophia in Istanbul served as a church, mosque, museum, and mosque again
Officially changed its international name from Turkey to Türkiye in 2022

items.prompt: The nineteenth most populous country at ~84 million, this Western European nation is the continent's most populous and its largest economy. Name it.
items.explanation:
#19 most populous country — ~84 million (most populous country in Europe, excluding Russia)
Europe's largest economy and the world's third-largest by nominal GDP
Capital Berlin; divided into East and West from 1949 to 1990
Reunified on October 3, 1990, after the fall of the Berlin Wall (November 9, 1989)
Federal republic of 16 states (Bundesländer)
Birthplace of the Protestant Reformation (Martin Luther, 1517) and the printing press (Gutenberg, c. 1440)

items.prompt: The twentieth most populous country at ~72 million, this Southeast Asian kingdom is the only nation in the region never colonized by a European power. Name it.
items.explanation:
#20 most populous country — ~72 million
Formerly known as Siam; renamed Thailand in 1939 (means 'Land of the Free')
Only Southeast Asian country never colonized by a European power
Capital Bangkok; official ceremonial name is 168 characters long, the world's longest place name
Constitutional monarchy; King Bhumibol Adulyadej (r. 1946–2016) was the world's longest-reigning monarch at the time of his death
World's largest exporter of rice for much of the late 20th century
